response:
Sec. 1  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A'S OFFER BECOMES MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B'S, WE COMPARE THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM EACH PUBLISHER.]

Sec. 2  
[THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM PUBLISHER A IS REPRESENTED BY THE EXPRESSION 3000 PLUS 0.05 TIMES THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE X.]

Sec. 3  
[THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM PUBLISHER B IS REPRESENTED BY THE EXPRESSION 1500 PLUS 0.10 TIMES THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE X.]

Sec. 4  
[TO FIND WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE SET UP THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 5  
[SOLVING THIS INEQUALITY INVOLVES SUBTRACTING 1500 FROM BOTH SIDES TO GET 1500 + 0.05X > 0.10X.]

Sec. 6  
[SUBTRACTING 0.05X FROM BOTH SIDES YIELDS 1500 > 0.05X, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO X < 30000.]

Sec. 7  
[THE RANGE OF SALES REVENUE WHERE PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE IS X LESS THAN 30000.]

Sec. 8  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED RANGE OF 10000 TO 40000, PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL FOR SALES REVENUE BETWEEN 10000 AND 30000.]
